text.title: Technical Debt Management Core Rules
text.summary: Six-step technical-debt discipline (register -> score impact via interest × contagion / effort -> allocate capacity -> pay down -> prevent -> track) shared between PM and engineering with a quarterly capacity contract.

rule.statement: Technical-debt register MUST tag each item by type: design / code / test / infra / docs / dependency.
rule.rationale: Mixed-type registers produce wrong paydown strategies.

rule.statement: Impact score MUST be (interest_per_month × contagion_factor) / paydown_effort. Single-axis scoring (effort-only) is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Effort-only scoring sorts by ease-of-fix, not by impact.

rule.statement: PM + engineering MUST agree on a written capacity contract (% of sprint capacity for debt) per quarter. 'As time permits' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Soft allocations get consumed by feature work.

rule.statement: Each paydown item MUST ship with a prevention policy (linter, code-review rule, CI gate) preventing reintroduction.
The debt register MUST be visible to stakeholders (exec, sales, support) — not engineering-private.
rule.rationale: Hidden debt traps PMs between exec frustration and engineering reality.

rule.statement: If applicability conditions fail (any Skip-If trigger holds — greenfield <3 months; throwaway prototype; tracer-bullet methodology; recent contract without trigger), the agent MUST skip this methodology and route to the named alternative or to the methodology fallback.
rule.rationale: Mis-applying a methodology outside its preconditions degrades output quality and pollutes downstream artefacts; the explicit skip-rule keeps the routing tree honest.
